purpose: Review the player_history_production_feature_v0 contract shape proposed in PR #124 and decide whether to accept, amend, or reject it as a formal contract proposal for a later implementation slice. Governance/contract-review only; does not authorize production binding.
decision_rationale: The core shape from PR #124 is structurally sound (temporal-cutoff, null/missing-history, and non-advice statements are correct and non-negotiable as originally written). Four fields were underspecified enough to invite drift: contract_version had no bump policy, provenance_state and validation_status were free text instead of closed enums, and run_id described what it should tie together but not how. These amendments are incorporated directly into this artifact, so no further amendment pass is required before a future implementation-design issue may be opened. Unresolved items (see unresolved_items) are legitimate scope decisions for that future issue, not contract defects.

field_classification.note: Accepted as-is: player_history_production_feature_v0. Fixed identifier; a new feature gets a new contract_id, not a version bump.
Amended to a strict semver bump policy (see version_bump_policy). Pre-1.0 until an implementation-design issue proposes 1.0.0.
Shape accepted as-is; artifact_sha256 value stays a placeholder, locked only at implementation-design time (unresolved item 1).
Accepted as-is: player_id, season, season_type, position. Changing these is a MAJOR bump.
Accepted as-is and locked. Target season S uses only seasons < S; no partial-season substitution.
requires and no_partial_season_substitution accepted and locked; family_scope (production-only vs full set) deferred to implementation design (unresolved item 2).
Accepted as-is and locked. No-history players get an entirely null feature block; never zero-coerced.
Amended from free text to a closed enum (see provenance_state_enum).
Accepted as-is: ISO-8601 timestamp of generation, not consumption.
New field, not present in the PR #124 proposal. Added because run_id derives from it and must be recomputable from a readable field on the contract instance.
Amended to a specified composition rule (see run_id_composition_rule) hashing the full source identity (path + sha256 + promotion_review), not artifact_sha256 alone.
Amended from free text to a closed enum (see validation_status_enum).
Accepted as-is and locked; must be preserved verbatim by every consumer.

forbidden_fields: any active-roster, availability, injury, depth-chart, or ownership-status field
any advice, ranking, score, recommendation, start/sit, trade, or draft-output field
any Fantasy consumer/route/UI/export identifier or reference
a production acceptance threshold value embedded in the contract schema itself
a literal 0 (or any other sentinel) standing in for missing in any numeric field -- missing is null, always

version_bump_policy.major: remove a field, change a field's type, or change a field's semantic meaning
version_bump_policy.patch: documentation/description change only, no behavioral change
version_bump_policy.pre_acceptance_note: any bump stays pre-1.0 until a future implementation-design issue proposes 1.0.0 acceptance

amended_contract_shape_reference.note: Shape reference only, not implemented as code or a runtime-validated schema in this PR.

source_dataset_refs.artifact_sha256: <locked by the implementation-design issue that instantiates this contract>
source_dataset_refs.promotion_review: <TIBER-Data promotion PR reference, locked at the same time>

temporal_cutoff_semantics.rule: feature values for target season S are built ONLY from seasons < S
temporal_cutoff_semantics.input_window: rolling N prior seasons (N deferred to the implementation-design issue; 3 in the #112/#122 experiments)
temporal_cutoff_semantics.excluded: target season S in any form, including partial-season in-progress data

feature_availability_rules.requires: at least one prior-season REG record for this player_id in the input window
feature_availability_rules.family_scope: DEFERRED to the implementation-design issue: production-only vs full feature set

null_missing_history_rules.no_history_player: entire feature block is null, never zero-filled, never imputed at serve time using post-hoc population statistics
null_missing_history_rules.unavailable_usage_fields: remain null even when history exists, exactly as in the source artifact

amended_contract_shape_reference.generated_at: ISO-8601 timestamp of generation, not of consumption
amended_contract_shape_reference.generator_script_version: version/revision identifier of the generator script that produced this instance
amended_contract_shape_reference.non_advice_non_ranking_statement: This contract describes a candidate MODEL FEATURE only. It is not fantasy advice, not a ranking, not a start/sit recommendation, and not a product-facing claim. No consumer of this contract may present its values, or any derivative of them, as advice or ranking output without a separate, explicitly-approved product-integration review.

non_negotiables_locked: target season S may only use seasons < S -- no exceptions
no target-season or partial-target-season substitution, under any feature-availability fallback
missing-history players must not be silently zero-filled -- the entire feature block stays null
source artifact identity must be path + sha256 + promotion review, never sha256 alone
a source-identity mismatch at generation time must fail closed, not degrade silently
the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled validation framing remains required for any future acceptance-threshold proposal
this review does not authorize seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
any Fantasy/product consumer requires its own separate, explicitly-approved review
unresolved_items: exact artifact sha lock for the specific promoted-artifact snapshot an implementation would use
exact feature-family scope: production-only (#116 attribution: joined MAE 40.173) vs full feature set (40.034)
final rolling-window length (N prior seasons; 3 in the #112/#122 experiments)
production acceptance threshold (not set by this review or PR #124)
leakage audit for the specific production inference path (broader than experimental leakage discipline)
deterministic implementation replay command (distinct from the experimental replay commands in PR #124 section 8)
missing-history subgroup reporting requirement at serve time
human sign-off authority for future model wiring (who, specifically, has authority over seasonalPprModel.ts)
non_goals: does not accept the contract for production implementation -- only for a future implementation-design issue to be opened against it
does not lock source_dataset_refs.artifact_sha256 or promotion_review to concrete values
does not set a production acceptance threshold
does not create, modify, or reference any TypeScript type, runtime schema, or validation code
does not approve a Fantasy consumer, UI surface, ranking, or advice behavior
does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact
does not modify seasonalPprModel.ts or any other production Forecast file

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future implementation issue invents its own provenance_state/validation_status values ad hoc
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: amendments.provenance_state_enum and amendments.validation_status_enum close both fields to fixed enums

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future implementation issue bumps contract_version inconsistently
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: amendments.version_bump_policy defines the bump policy explicitly

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future implementation issue treats run_id as any unique string, or hashes source sha256 alone and collapses distinct promoted-source identities
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: amendments.run_id_composition_rule specifies the exact composition over the full source identity (path + sha256 + promotion_review) plus contract_version, generator_script_version, and generated_at

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future implementation issue silently decides the feature-family scope without review
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: unresolved_items keeps this an open, named decision

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future implementation issue treats this review as production-bound authorization
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: provenance_state_enum makes production_bound unreachable by this review or the next issue alone

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: a future implementation issue bundles a Fantasy consumer with contract implementation
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: non_negotiables_locked restates that consumer approval is never the same decision as contract acceptance

next_step: The next_step_decision permits opening a SEPARATE implementation-design issue scoped to unresolved_items. That issue must still clear every gate in PR #124 section 6 before any code touching a production feature path may be written. This document does not open that issue and does not itself satisfy any gate beyond the contract-shape review captured here.
